EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 59 mph was recorded at the Milford Municipal Airport ASOS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Southwest flow ushered in an increase in moisture with a low pressure trough and strong cold front moving through. This resulted in moderate to heavy snow across the Elkheads and Park Range Mountains with moderate snow over portions of the central and southern Colorado mountains and eastern Uinta Mountains. Temperatures were mild throughout much of this event, limiting snow accumulations at lower elevations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Generally 4 to 8 inches of snow fell across the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early September moisture surge resulted in the return of a more active weather pattern to Utah, including some isolated instances of flash flooding as well as a strong thunderstorm wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A rapid rise from a no-flow state to around 300 cfs was observed on the Paria River Near Kanab, UT gauge along US-89.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early September moisture surge resulted in the return of a more active weather pattern to Utah, including some isolated instances of flash flooding as well as a strong thunderstorm wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall resulted in the development of a flash flood and associated flood wave in Hurricane Wash. An emergency manager reported that a hiker within the wash was carried approximately 300 feet downstream before climbing a tree to escape the flood. Due to injuries, the hiker required helicopter evacuation for medical care. There were also washouts reported on Hole-in-the-Rock Road with seven vehicles reported stranded. Time of flood estimated based on radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early September moisture surge resulted in the return of a more active weather pattern to Utah, including some isolated instances of flash flooding as well as a strong thunderstorm wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain in the head waters of the Dirty Devil resulted in the generation of a flood wave. The river gage on the Dirty Devil measured a rapid spike in flow from a no-flow state to a flow of 727 cfs over the course of 30 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early September moisture surge resulted in the return of a more active weather pattern to Utah, including some isolated instances of flash flooding as well as a strong thunderstorm wind gust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The I-15 @ Plymouth mesonet station recorded a wind gust of 58 mph as a result of outflow winds associated with a nearby cluster of showers and thunderstorms moving through the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A multi-day surge of moisture associated with a slow moving broad synoptic trough resulted in a period of unsettled weather, including instances of heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a near stationary storm in central Emery County resulted in radar estimated water totals in the 2 to 5 inch range over the span of around one to two hours. This rain fell within the catchment area for the Saleratus Wash. A flood wave form and propagated downstream towards the Green River for the next several hours. This flood wave eventually reached an unprotected crossing at Airport Road in the town of Green River. This led to the necessity of recue operations in this location for an individual who was brought to safety, though they required treatment for minor injuries at the hospital afterwards. The heavy rain fell late in the morning, with the Emery County EM estimating the flood wave's downstream impacts occurred around 4 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching trough helped provide enough synoptic support in combination with available moisture to trigger isolated to scattered convection across the northern half of Utah or so. A few of these thunderstorms produced strong to severe wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The CAUSEWAY mesonet station recorded a wind gust of 62 mph as a result of outflow winds associated with a nearby cluster of showers and thunderstorms moving through the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching trough helped provide enough synoptic support in combination with available moisture to trigger isolated to scattered convection across the northern half of Utah or so. A few of these thunderstorms produced strong to severe wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The I-80 at MP 71 East Delle mesonet station recorded a wind gust of 63 mph as a result of outflow winds associated with a nearby cluster of showers and thunderstorms moving through the area.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Hansel Valley mesonet station recorded a wind gust of 59 mph as a result of outflow winds associated with a nearby cluster of showers and thunderstorms moving through the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fairly weak cutoff low slowly ejected northward through the area over the course of several days. This feature in combination with sufficient moisture created an environment that supported several days of shower and thunderstorm development. Some of these storms produced locally heavy rainfall and flash flooding, as well as some strong wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall resulted in a flood wave around 140 cfs (1 foot rise) hitting the Fort Pierce gauge.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A flash flood occurred in Little Wildhorse Canyon due to heavy rainfall in an area which drains into the narrow canyon. This flash flood trapped multiple groups, forcing these groups to seek higher ground. Search and rescue was notified, and eventually all parties were able to hike out safely.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain in the Paria River drainage resulted in the development and propagation of a flood wave. Approximately 2 miles below the White House campground a group of hikers required helicopter evacuation.
